subject: re: Paano ba
body: Gusto kong intindihin yung katwiran mo kaso sobrang babaw. Nagtatrabaho ako sa ibang bansa. Inayos ko lahat ng legal documents ko dito. Halos nasa 15k lang nagastos ko kasama na pamasahe at pagkain. Hindi katwiran yung hindi maibigay yung hinihingi kasi looking at your argument mukhang tamad ka lang magasikaso. Wala namang problema sa paghahanap ng trabaho sa ibang bansa eh. Ang point lang namin dumaan kayo sa tamang proseso at magapply sa tamang ahensya. Para sainyo din naman yan para maayos ang record niyo at malakas ang kapit sa gobyerno kung sakaling magkaproblema. Kasi kung kayo sa sarili niyo di kayo marunong sumunod sa tama asahan niyong makakarma din kayo dyan sa pamamagitan ng ibang bagay. Hindi kayo magiging sa successful sa paggawa ng mali guys always remember that.

Actually if I have the resources nga lang sana, I want to go as far as filing a case, but the smarter move would be just to let it go and let karma bite that IO in the a**!

subject: Second Immigration Officer
body: I forgot to mention din nga pala, it was not only the IO in the window desk who asked me questions, she had to refer me to another IO, had me answer a questionnaire in the office near the window desks, and that's where my nightmare began, I think that IO holds a higher position than those who stays at the window desks, he holds the final say whether those referred to him can board their planes or not. And you're right it's really the confidence that matters, it's just that, that was the first time that happened to me, sobrang nabigla ako, it felt humiliating, I also kept on looking at my watch kasi I think that happened 30 minutes my supposed boarding and that f*****g doesn't seem to care!

subject: I'm usually very confident...
body: I'm usually very confident, with my English speaking ability, I'm not boasting but I can say I am a fluent speaker, I was actually answering most of his questions in English but man oh man, that IO really made me lose the confidence in me especially with his tone and the manner of how he spoke, I mean there's a big difference between being a professional who's just doing your job and being just flatout mean and a power-tripper. What others have been saying about IOs are true, so sad I had to find out the hard way. Any idea what would happen to me next time I take on another trip abroad? Do you think they will be as strict to me as I have already a record?

subject: Agree ako dun sa comment sa baba haha
body:
Agree ako dun sa sinabi nung isa. Madaming IO mahilig mangpowertrip (paulit ulit na tanong, mabusisi sa documents, paikot ikot tanong) pero yun kasi ang effective way para hulihin if nagsisinungaling ba yung tao. I don't get it bakit ka kakabahan if wala ka namang gagawing mali? I mean hindi mo ba kayang sumagot ng maayos and be confident? Kasi kahit tourist ka talaga pero barok ka magsalita kahit sino magdududa sayo. Siguro bago ka magprepare ng documents mas mabuti kung sarili mo muna ihanda mo. Build your confidence. Learn English and speak it fluently and most importantly, WAG NA WAG KANG MATATAKOT SA IO. 

Based on my experience ganito madalas scenario ko sa harap ng IO:
IO: san ka pupunta? (while looking at my ticket and passport)
Me: (name of country)
IO: Sino kasama mo?
Me: I'll be travelling solo po sir/maam
IO: Bakit ka pupunta dun?
Me: I just wanna visit the country's historical sites (I like answering it specifically to my purpose - concerts, tourist attractions, aattend ng event, etc.)
IO: Saan mo nalaman about dyan? (I find it weird kapag tinatanong to pero just answer it anyway)
Me: History Channel (HAHAHAH ODIBA PASOK)
......
IO: (tatak passport and picture time na)
Me: Thank you sir/maam!

Odiba? Basta wag ka lang kabahan. Madalas ako matapat sa IO na masungit kahit anong iwas ko haha pero palagi ko lang iniisip na wala silang magagawa para harangin ako kaya just answer them ng maayos and you'll be fine.

subject: FIRST TIME TO TRAVEL ABROAD (Europe)
body:
I want to share what happened when my friend and I travelled to Europe last June 17.

NAIA Terminal 1 kami. Second time ko na aalis ng bansa while yung friend ko eh first time pa lang. As in bago at wala pang tatak passport nya. Dahil medyo wala syang alam pa pagdating namin ng immigration, sya ang pinapila ko at ako na ang kumuha nang card (embarkation card ba yun) at nagfill out para sa aming dalawa. Employed ako at 1 month pa lang sa work dahil kakaresign ko pa lang sa previous job ko while this friend of mine is unemployed. 

Pagdating sa immigration, sa kasamaang palad pa natapat kami sa babae na mabusisi at mukhang mataray, hindi na ako masyado tinanong. Magkasama kaming pumunta sa IO dahil gusto ko na aalalayan ko ang friend ko na first timer. Ayoko na mag isa lang sya dahil baka magisa sya. So ako din nag abot ng mga passport namin pati nung cards.

(My case) 
IO: Saan  ka pupunta? At sino pupuntahan mo dun?
Ako: Sa switzerland po bibisitahin namin yung tita ng friend ko.
IO: may work ka? Ano work mo? 
Ako: (stated my job) 
IO: Gaano kana katagal sa work mo?
Ako: 1 month po.
IO: 1 month ka palang?
Ako: Opo

At ayun hiningi nya lang company ID ko at tinignan nya. Then tatak na. Pagkatatak at bigay ng passport ko, di ako umalis at nagbantay pa rin ako sa friend ko. Tinanong lang naman sya kung papano nya naging tita yung pupuntahan namin at para masatisfy yung IO, binigay namin yung Authenticated Affidavit of Support na pinadala ng tita nya from Switzerland. Kinuha yun ng tita nya mula sa Philippine embassy sa Bern.

Binusising maige nung IO yung affidavit at pagkatapos, tinatakan na din yung passport nya. Nakaalis kami kahit wala syang work at 1 month palang ako sa current job ko.

Ang advice ko, wag magpapasindak. Wag matatakot at magdasal nang maige. Presence of mind at wag na wag ka magsisinungaling kung hindi mo kayang back upan yung mga di totoong sasabihin mo. Kung may kasama kayong aalis, iassess nyo ang inyong mga sarili kung sino ang mukhang magiging tagilid pagdating sa IO at maging ready na yung kasama na tulungan yung tagilid nyang companion.

subject: First time/solo traveler

subject: EXCELLENT SERVICE FROM ANGELITO ABIQUE
body:
Angelito Abique - Palawan Airport Tourist Police Officer should be highly commended for his excellent service and support.  I had purchased a Philipino Askrima weapon and was not able to carry onboard.  The tourist Police Officer at the Palawan airport, Angelito Abique offered to post the stick to an address in Manila where I could retrieve it before boarding my flight home to Australia. 
This wonderful man posted the stick the same day as promised and even sent the tracking number so we could be sure.  
Thankyou so much for the excellent service and support.  I have found all the Filipinos to be very helpful and polite.  A great country and a great people.
I visited in June 2017 and i will certainly return.

Leanne Gunn - Melbourne Vic Australia

Ang dadalhin ko is ung invitation letter, bank statement nya, passport stamps na nagpunta na sya dito at nagspend ng time with my family at talagang magkasama kami even sa Singapore, tapos bank statement nya at payslip, pocket money in $$ po, taposung pictures kase may record na kami sa pagkakaalam ko kase ung IO nung first time namin umalis together nagprint cla ng pictures namin together,  tapos ung passport na din nya for identification. Tama na po ba yan?

subject: Give me a break!
body: Food for thought: What would happen if you ever needed an emergency flight by the Royal Flying Doctor Service or Air Ambulance or Care Flight.  These aircraft aren't manufactured with pilots in them, no, they have to do lots training both on the ground and airborne and whilst you might think the pilot flying overhead is annoying he or she just might save your life or that of one of your family one day.  So instead of lambasting them for upsetting your peace and quiet say a little prayer of thanks that they are doing something worthwhile with their lives.  Next time you take off in a jumbo jet there's a real human being up front, in fact two, one is a pilot and the other is his co-pilot and without them the aircraft doesn't take off.  When there are no aircraft flying that's the time to start worrying.

subject: re: re: RE:Source of income
body: Siguradong hindi maghahanap ng papel yun. kasi nga pag may buyer kayo maghahanap ng seller. pag nakakita kayo ng seller sasabihin mo sa seller ihahanap kita ng buyer . Tapos tatanong mo yung porsinto mo. Kailangan pa ba ng papel. Yung mag seller at buyer lang ang magkakroon ng papel. Halimbawa may nag bebenta ng bakal 1 tonelada sabihin mo sa seller may buyer ako...Magkano porsiyento ko? Tapos pag nakahanap ka ng buyer na kailangan niya ng bakal sabihin mo sa kanya ihahanap kita ng nag bebenta ng bakal. pag nakahanap ka magkano porsiyento mo? Yun ang source of income mo.

subject: re:cross country
body: Pag cross country medyo mahirap...ang pinka malapit na bansa ay Malaysia, at port of entry ay sa Sandakan.  sakay kayo ng barko. Kung gusto ninyo ng illigal mas kailangan ng maraming pera. maraming lagusan...Mula sa Palawan sa bayan ng Btaraza ay sakay ng motor boat..papuntang Pulau Banggi.. at dapat madaling araw ang biyahe kasi nga maraming coast Gurad...(exciting naman). pag naman Zamboanga punta kayo Basilan, tapos sa Jolo, tapos sa Tawi tawi tapos nun sa Semporna, malaysia. Kailangan na ka back pack lang kayo. kasi nga malayong lakrin at medyo adventure. pero at least yun ang tinatawag na cross country. ang meaning ng cross country sa diksonaryo ay  proceeding over countryside (as across fields and through woods) and not by roads. wala kasi tayong border kaya hindi matatwag na cross country.

subject: TRAVEL ADVISORY TO SINGAPORE
body:
The Embassy wishes to remind our kababayans who had been forcibly removed from Singapore and are planning to re-enter Singapore, including transiting passengers, to seek prior clearance from the Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA) before finalizing their travel plans.

It is incumbent upon our concerned kababayans to declare truthfully the information being requested in the embarkation and disembarkation card.

Unlawful return after removal from Singapore is punishable under Section 36 of the Immigration Act and violation thereof shall be punished with imprisonment ranging tom one to three years, liable to a fine not exceeding S$6,000, and, in addition, be liable to be removed from Singapore.

subject: Bribery at the airport - it's disgusting!
body: My bag had a sticker saying it needs to be inspected.  The a-officer took me to a quite area and turned on his scanning machine.  He asked to see my passport and showed me the 15 or so jars of Manuka honey I had with me through the scanning image.  He said I need to pay taxes for the honey jars or that I can hand him a few dollars and he would let me through.  As this was my first time back in Vietnam, I was nervous of the whole corruption process so decided to give the a-officer a $5 to get moving.  It's a disgrace to see how corruption is still not dealt with right in an international airport.  The experience ruins it all for me in ever wanting to go back.

subject: re: Read
body: I really appreciate this guy! Tama lahat ng sinabi niya. Just be honest regarding the purpose of your travel. Immigration officers are actually there to help us and protect us from potential Human Traffickers. MArami kase satin sinungaling, sasabihin magtotour lang un pala either mag hahanap ng work or meron ng trabaho na nag aantay na hindi dumaan sa POEA. What if may mangyaring masama sayo abroad? pano ka hahanapin at tutulungan ng gobyerno kung hindi mismo alam ng obyerno natin na nagtatrabaho ka pala doon? san ka nila hahanapin? sino ang papanagutin ng goyerno? wala diba?

subject: 3rd time to leave PH, working freelance
body:
First time I left 2015 was with my sister and two other cousins. The flight was MNL-KL-JOG-KL-MNL. I was still a student at that time, I showed my school ID and Certificate of Registration, and I was with my sister so she vouched for all the payments made from her credit card. STAMPED. The second time I left was 2016. The flight was MNL-SGN-REP-MNL. I paid for my all my bookings through ECPay (711), booked hotels through Booking.com, was also flying with a friend so we went through immigration together. The lady IO asked me the nature of my job (Project Supervisor at a firm), then asked me for my company ID, COE, and LOA. Honest to God, I didn't know these things were necessary. So I told her I left all of the above at home. So she asked me for Gov't IDs (TIN, SSS, PhilHealth card in particular) but my company at that did not provide us any of the above. So I said "probationary" pa lang ako sa work ko kaya hindi pa nila ako nabibigyan. (Been working for more than a year there pero walang kahit anong benefits and we don't pay taxes kasi wala kaming SSS, Pag-ibig, etc. I know dapat sinumbong ko sila sa DOLE but I really LOVED my job and kindness got the better of me. Eventually, I quit.) At this point kabado nako kasi unang labas ko everything was a breeze. She was not convinced, so she asked me our itinerary in Saigon and Siem Reap (which I know by heart because I planned everything: budget, time, places to go, etc.) And also I pointed out to my friend sa kabilang IO cubicle. Then, STAMPED.

Now, I'm leaving PH alone but meeting a friend (babae at Pinoy din) in Hanoi. She will be coming from Dubai because she works there and is only on one-week leave. My friends tell me I shouldn't be paranoid because I've already been to 4 countries. But some IOs, I heard, can power trip really hard. Mas okay na mag-overprepare. Hope you guys can help me out!

subject: Ingelara airstrip
body: Today June 5 2017 I took off from Ingelara airstrip. I know I have written here before but I have to say a very big thank you to Stuart and Cheree Ogg who are wonderful people in allowing us aviators the opportunity to fly to Carnarvon Gorge, without their generosity  it would not be possible to do so. The airstrip is in terrific condition ideal for my C182. There is a windsock and the elevation is 1100 feet.I believe they should charge a landing fee but what ever they get is given to the RFDS. I think a minimum of $50 is appropriate. Please remember Rewan is now closed and on any case Ingelara is far superior in my opinion and I have been flying for 57 years. Thank you Stuart and Cheree.    Peter Pigott

subject: Just to be Confirmed for UAE Visa
body:
Philippines and Pakistan passport holders especially who are in the UAE are in risk of being stranded due to the halt of 90-days Visa issuance by the UAE government.
Consul General Paul Raymund Cortes also confirmed about the halt of 90-day Visa processing and he said he also has no knowledge on the reason why they stopped it.
The Filipino Times visited various travel agencies whom also confirmed that the  rule has been made effective  starting June 1, 2017.

body:
Average Daily Cost Per person, per day AED1,061 
One Week Per person AED7,424 
In malls you will find all kinds of restaurants. Most American fast foods are present in Dubai – KFC, Chillis, TGI Fridays Starbucks and McDonalds. The cheapest option is shawarma and falafel, which cost about 3.75 AED (0.6£). Traditional Shawarma can be found at almost every corner in Dubai. Also taste the falafel, which is at about the same price. 
Budget lunch – shawarma or western fast food for a price range of 11 – 34 AED

subject: Where is this all coming from?
body:
I'm reading all the negative comments and can't understand where it is all coming from...

I've been doing my flight training at Lilydale for the past year and it has been nothing but a great experience! Best value for money training that I could find in the greater Melbourne area. They have friendly and proficient instructors and staff. No landing fees, short taxi ways and you don't have to wait ages for clearance.

I would highly recommend a visit!

subject: What a shame!
body:
Restaurant is great, and with renovations complete and the new school onsite, airport is now bee-hive of activity - but what was once a beautiful and accessible airport in cottage country has become a money grab for management (Loomex)
Price for fuel is also very high. There are no more GA friendly airport gestures ...including (but certainly not limited to) no more overnight tie down waived with fuel, no more waiving or reducing tie-down for aircraft unexpectedly grounded with mechanical issues or weathered in. Hangars are also nice but extremely expensive.  
Everyone originally thought that the increase in volume would help keep costs down, but with Loomex (operating at their healthy margin) now involved with a back-office load of people, almost everything has a new (2x) cost. Anything remaining that does not - soon will. Same story at Lindsay but without renovations or a flying school.

subject: Success!
body: I just passed the Immigration just NOW I'm going to Phuket, Thailand. It's my first time traveling abroad alone. Female. 22yrs old. Unemployed. Punta ako dun for vacation with my boyfriend na manggagaling Australia. We already met. Sagot ko ticket ko round trip sa accommodation siya sumagot. Anyway, about sa Immigration I'm really shaking! Specially my voice hahaha Pero Ayun, pinakita ko lang tix ko and accommodation. And pictures ng bf ko and all his information (copy ng passport ni bf, ticket info nya Tska birthday!! ) . Tapos since 22 lang ako tinanong kung student ako sabi ko Undergrad Tas tinanong kung saang school What course. Hinahanap kasi yung student ID ko which is hindi ko dala Tas chineck nlng yung old ID ko sa prv Work ko. Tas ayun tinatakan na. Nag advice lang na Next time dalhin  ko pa rin daw yung student ID ko kahit undergrad as reference. Actually wala pang 5mins yung interview and babaeng officer pa yun. Just be positive lang guys. And pray!!! It really works.

subject: Meeting Australian....for the first time.....
body:
Hi guys, share ko Lang experience ko nung May 15th 2017, Pa Australia ako to meet my Australian friend, never pa kame nag meet first time traveller din ako, Male 23 y.o.

Ayun, sobrang over prepared ako, i have every documents na pwede nila hingin.

Tapos nung nasa immigration ako. Binigay ko Lang passport. Boarding pass and Departure card(nilagay ko work ko dun mas ok pag may work ka and purpose is travel/holiday).

Tapos hiningi nya Visa Grant ko medyo seryoso kuno boses nia, tapos nung tinitingan niya visa grant ko may pag iling-iling pa sya na may kasamang tsk-tsk, dipadin ako kinabahan kase alam kong artista sila at palabas lang nila yun, wala naman kaseng kailing iling sa visa grant ko haha, themln ayun Tinanong pati sakin kung kailan ako babalik and kung may kakilala ako dun sabi ko "Yup, friend ko" :)

Tapos before nya ko tanungin pipicturan ka pala dun, tinutok ni kase sakin ung camera, sabi ko "pwede mag smile ung labas ung teeth?" Hahaha 
Tapos natawa lang sya, sabi nya ay ok nadaw, na stamp'an na passport ko :)

Be confident lang guys and smile kayo.
Sobrang kalma lang ako nun, ineexpect ko kakabahan ako ng bongga. kaso mas lamang excitement ko mag travel kesa kabahan ehehe, first time ko kase mag travel.

subject: SUCCESS!
body:
Hey everyone! Gusto ko lang ishare, nandito na ako sa NAIA 3 waiting for my flight bound to Japan. First time ko lalabas ng bansa at mag isa lang ako. Medyo madami yung tao. Girl ako kaya as usual, as per advice ng marami, sa guy ka na IO pumila. So una kong ginawa nag fill out muna ako na Departure Card before pumila. Then nung turn ko na kay kuyang immigration, Cuerdo yung surname nya, inabot ko yung passport ko and ticket to Japan. 
IO: san ka pupunta?
Ako: sa Japan
IO: first time mo abroad?
Ako: oo 
IO: licensed accountant ka?
Ako: hindi
IO: san ka accountant? 
Ako: (stated the name of company) 
IO: pahiram ng company ID
Ako:(inabot ko yung company ID ko then binalik nya ulit)
IO: sino kasama mo?
Ako: wala
IO: sino pupuntahan mo dun?
Ako: wala
IO: okay patingin ng return ticket.
Ako: (handed him yung boarding pass ko na printed nung nag web check in ako)
IO: kailan ka babalik? (While scanning my return trip boarding pass) 
Ako: May 18

Ayun stamp na. Tapos inabot na nya passport ko and yung ticket and boarding pass ko. 

Napansin ko lang, dapat talaga di ka magkamali sa pagsagot at dire diretso ang pagsagot.Wag mo din tatantanan ng eye contact. And then less talk less mistake nga.  

Yung nasa unahan ko na maglola, hindi nakago agad kasi may hiningi si IO na hindi naibigay kesyo nasa luggage daw nila na nakacheck in na. Ayun pinakuha ni IO at sinabihan na bumalik sa kanya.  Nga pala, I am only wearing pants, polo shirt at rubber shoes. Nakamake up din ako ng konti, binonggahan ko lang ang lipstick ko para di halata just in case mamutla ako. Nakaback pack lang din ako.  Ayun, presence of mind lang talaga ang kailangan and be prepared. Wag papangunahan ng kaba and of course pray hard! 

Good luck everyone!

subject: Hongkong
body: I went to hongkong just this may 1, it is my second travel outside the country. The immigration officer, didn't even ask any question or ask any documents. He just stamped it. But I already have complete documents in me just in case they'll ask for it. This also happened when I first travelled to thailand, no questions asked. To all travelers, prepare all pertinent documents, kelangan man or Hindi. Do research what you need. Do no rely on someone else to do it for you. Confidence too, and eye contact is very important.

We the pilots at Old Park would be very happy if they would stop as it annoys us too. Try contacting  the officer commanding at St Athan and asking them to stop! We have and were told because its open  airspace they can do what they want.And that is true.However if you do have an issue with the flying at Old Park call Rob on 07989537508. Then we can address the problem.  The last thing we want is unhappy neighbours!

subject: Closed in 1974
body:
Nicosia Airport was closed in 1974, and now sits on a UN base in the Cyprus Buffer Zone. Access can be obtained only if you have a pass for the base, which won't be granted just to visit the airport, you need a reason to be there.
there
I have visited briefly. The building still exists and everything is left as it was when it was abandoned, there's even a derelict Air Cyprus Hawker-Siddeley Trident still there (only the shell, it's be thoroughly gutted). 
Although listed as closed, the airport is used as the HQ of the UN Peacekeeping Force in Cyprus and very rare fixed wing UN flights take place. It could be argued that it is still actively used as a Heliport as active UN helicopters are based here.

subject: @Going to Bangkok
body:
If may company ID kapa keep it, in case the IO will ask you may mapapakita ka.
Mag ready ka ng COE or Approved VL or Leave of Absence. I just made mine pero if you know your previous work well, wala nmn idea si IO na unemployed ka, in case magtanong si IO about your work.
Save all the receipts sa Airline and sa hotel. Gawa ka din ng itinerary para mag mukhang legit ang tour nyo. It's better na maging over prepare than kulang kapag humarap kana kay IO and keep it in mind be confident and don't overthink things. Ask ko lang ano pala gender mo and ng friend mo?

subject: Success!
body:
Hello po sa lahat

Share ko lang experience namin kahapon.

Dito na po ako at ang kasama ko sa Bangkok, sa May 4 going kami Dubai. 

Sa NAIA Terminal 2 kami after check in we fixed ourselves before we presented our self sa IO. Here's my convo with an IO.

IO: Patingin ng return ticket
Me: Ah! Ito po ba yun?
IO: First time mo lumabas ng country?
Me: Yes (Though i have been to Saudi na before) pero gamit new passport
IO: Where do you work?
Me: i said the company sabay dugtong na Call center.  Pero i dont work there anymore as a matter of fact i am unemployed pero naka ready ang Company ID and COE and Approved Leave of Absence.
IO: *Stamp *Stamp

I think nakatulong yung nagbihis kami and we presented ourselves well sa IO. Wala din pila

subject: Trolleys not available
body:
As Salaamu Alaikum:
For some reason trolleys are not available to the general public. The workers at the airport keep the trolleys and won't make them available to the general public unless you give them 10 Riyals. Trolleys aren't even available for the general public to take a trolley as needed. The Trolley Mafia will not even give you a trolley unless you give them 10 Riyals first. 
Now if someone needs a trolley and a worker this service should be available, however if a patron doesn't need a worker trolleys should be available also.
The airport in Yanbu has trolleys available for departures and arrivals inside and outside the airport terminal. Perhaps management should look into this situation.
I hope this could be resolved. Thank you very much.

subject: COY Apron
body:
The apron for YCOY is at the South Western end, there are 2 Cessna's that park up near the "bowser", there is a third (and maybe fourth) tie down directly behind them (old tyres and rope).
AVGAS is NOT available, closest available is YCAR roughly 100nm South (Self serve bowser, security controlled AP), call the guys at Ningaloo Reef Dive and talk to the Chief Pilot (currently Paul) for any info.

We flew in there in a 172 late March 2017, was a beautiful strip and an amazing approach, Enjoy!

subject: Visiting foreign bf - no problem on immigration
body:
Hi,  just like to share my experience.  

I'm 22 years old, single,  female, first time traveller nung umalis ako nung April 13 evening.  
I prepared everything,  invitiation letter,  coe,  (no bank statements, but i have access sa banks naman sa phone, i was thinking i can just log on and show them),  approved leaved,  visa.  For CFO,  i would recommend getting one,  super easy lang naman siya. Half day lang and after makuha mo siya at least wala ka na inaalala. Even tourist,  if you will ask the counselor they would say na you needed one.  It was also the last resort of IO if they doubt on you, hihingin nila yun.  It was never asked to me but i guess it is because i  present myself calmly and confidently sa IO (confident because i know complete ako lahat).  My destination was France.  So i have a valid schengen visa.  
IO: (scanning my passport,  maybe lookinh for stamps, then asked)  first time mo lalabas ng bansa? 
Me: Yes.  
IO: so Accountant ka?  Saan ka nagtratrabaho ngayon
Me: ****** (my company was very well known worldwide) 
IO: kailan ka babalik
Me: hmm,  May 5 I'll leave France,  but I'll arrive Manila on May 6. 
IO: *stamp stamp*
No documents was asked,  not even my company id.  Just be confident and stay true on your intentions why will you travel
